data.title: Dollar Gains on Euro and Stock Weakness
data.description:
The dollar index Friday rose by +0.21%. The dollar rose moderately on Friday due to weakness in the euro. EUR/USD is under pressure ahead of German elections this weekend. The dollar also found support on Friday’s sell-off in stocks, which boosted liquidity demand for the dollar.

The dollar fell back from its best level Friday after the University of Michigan US Feb consumer sentiment index was revised lower to a 15-month low, and after Jan existing home sales fell more than expected.

The US Feb S&P Global manufacturing PMI rose +0.4 to an 8-month high of 51.6, stronger than expectations of 51.4. However, the Feb S&P Global services PMI unexpectedly fell -3.2 to 49.7, weaker than expectations of an increase to 53.0 and the steepest pace of contraction in 2 years.

The University of Michigan US Feb consumer sentiment index was revised lower by -3.1 to a 15-month low of 64.7, weaker than expectations of no change at 67.8.

The University of Michigan US Feb 1-year inflation expectations remained unchanged at 4.3%, right on expectations. However, the Feb 5-10 year inflation expectations unexpectedly accelerated to a 29-year high of 3.5%, higher than expectations of no change at 3.3%.

US Jan existing home sales fell -4.9% m/m to 4.08 million, weaker than expectations of -2.6% to 4.13 million.

The markets are discounting the chances at 2% for a -25 bp rate cut at the next FOMC meeting on March 18-19.

EUR/USD Friday fell by -0.42%. The yen rallied to a 2-1/2 month high against the dollar Friday after Japan Jan consumer prices rose further above 2%, a hawkish factor for BOJ policy. The strength in the Japan Jan CPI report pushed the 10-year JGB bond yield to a 15-year high of 1.466%, strengthening the yen’s interest rate differentials. Gains in the yen accelerated on Friday as T-note yields trended lower.

The yen gave up its gains and turned lower briefly in the overnight session after BOJ Governor Ueda warned that the BOJ would boost its purchases of government bonds if long-term bond yields rose too quickly. 

Japan Jan national CPI rose +4.0% y/y, right on expectations and the fastest increase in 2 years. Jan national CPI ex-fresh food and energy rose +2.5% y/y, which is right on expectations and the fastest increase in 10 months.

The Japan Feb Jibun Bank manufacturing PMI rose +0.2 to 48.9.

BOJ Governor Ueda said, “Moves in bond yields fluctuate to a certain degree; however, we will purchase government bonds nimbly to foster the stable formation of yields in exceptional cases where long-term yields rise sharply.” 

April gold (GCJ25) Friday closed down -2.90 (-0.10%), and March silver (SIH25) closed down -0.474 (-1.42%). Precious metals on Friday posted moderate losses. Friday’s stronger dollar weighed on precious metals prices. Gold was also pressured on negative carryover from Thursday when Treasury Secretary Bessent dampened speculation that the US government might devalue its bullion holdings when he said it’s “not what I had in mind” when discussing plans for a sovereign wealth fund. 

Losses in precious metals Friday were limited by comments from BOJ Governor Ueda, who sparked demand for precious metals as a store of value when he said the BOJ would boost QE if long-term bond yields rose too quickly. Also, fund buying of gold supports prices as long gold positions in ETFs rose to a 13-month high Thursday. In addition, geopolitical tensions and concerns over President Trump’s trade and geopolitical agenda are boosting safe-haven demand for precious metals.

On the date of publication, Rich Asplund did not have (either directly or indirectly) positions in any of the securities mentioned in this article. All information and data in this article is solely for informational purposes. For more information please view the Barchart Disclosure Policyhere.

data.title: North Korea’s Lazarus Group responsible for ByBit hack resulting in losses of over $1.5 billion: Arkham
data.description:
Arkham Intelligence said Lazarus Group hacked ByBit for over $1.5 billion on Friday, citing information provided by online sleuth ZachXBT.

"At 19:09 UTC today, ZachXBT submitted definitive proof that this attack on ByBit was performed by the LAZARUS GROUP," Arkham said in a post to X. "His submission included a detailed analysis of test transactions and connected wallets used ahead of the exploit, as well as multiple forensics graphs and timing analyses. The submission has been shared with the ByBit team in support of their investigation."

Earlier in the day ByBit confirmed it had suffered a massive security breach that led to the loss of over $1.5 billion in crypto assets. The company's co-founder and CEO Ben Zhao later said that all client withdrawals will be processed, even if they are under review."

The hack is being considered the largest single theft in crypto's history.

data.title: Red Rock Resorts, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Red Rock Resorts, Inc., a prominent player in the Las Vegas regional gaming and entertainment market, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. This summary highlights the key aspects of the report, offering insights into the company's recent achievements and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Red Rock Resorts, Inc. reported robust financial performance for the year 2024, driven by strategic expansions and operational efficiencies:

  • Net Revenues: $1,939 million, reflecting a 12.5% increase from the previous year, primarily driven by the opening of the Durango property.
  • Operating Income: $568.7 million, a 1.8% increase from 2023, with the Durango property contributing significantly to this growth.
  • Net Income: $291.3 million, a decrease from $337.8 million in 2023, impacted by increased interest expenses and depreciation.
  • Net Income Attributable to Red Rock Resorts, Inc.: $154.1 million, down from $176.0 million in 2023, reflecting higher operating costs and interest expenses.
  • Earnings per share of Class A common stock, basic: $2.61, compared to $3.04 in 2023, influenced by increased expenses and interest costs.
  • Earnings per share of Class A common stock, diluted: $2.53, down from $2.94 in 2023, due to similar factors affecting basic EPS.

Business Highlights

Red Rock Resorts, Inc. continues to strengthen its position in the Las Vegas regional market through strategic operational initiatives:

  • Revenue Segments: The company's principal source of revenue is gaming, with approximately 80% of casino revenue generated from slot play. Non-gaming offerings include restaurants, hotels, and other entertainment amenities.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates primarily in the Las Vegas regional market, with properties strategically located throughout the Las Vegas valley. Over 90% of the Las Vegas population is located within five miles of one of the company's gaming facilities.
  • Sales Units: As of December 31, 2024, the company offered 16,447 slot machines, 320 table games, and 3,030 hotel rooms in the Las Vegas market.
  • New Product Launches: Durango Casino & Resort opened in December 2023, featuring approximately 83,000 square feet of casino space, four full-service food and beverage outlets, a food hall, a state-of-the-art sports book, and a resort-style pool.
  • New Production Launches: In January 2025, construction commenced to expand the casino floor and build an additional parking garage at the Durango property.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue focusing on operational efficiencies and cost control while exploring new development opportunities and potential acquisitions to maximize equityholder returns.

Strategic Initiatives

Red Rock Resorts, Inc. has undertaken several strategic initiatives to enhance its market position and financial stability:

  • Expansion and Development: The opening of the Durango Casino & Resort in December 2023 significantly contributed to the increase in net revenues for 2024. The company is also involved in the development of a gaming and entertainment facility with the North Fork Rancheria of Mono Indians, which is expected to enhance its presence in the Native American gaming market.
  • Capital Management: The company engaged in several capital management activities, including refinancing transactions that resulted in an amended and restated credit agreement. This included a new senior secured term 'B' loan facility of $1.57 billion and a new senior secured revolving credit facility of $1.1 billion. Additionally, Red Rock issued $500 million of 6.625% Senior Notes due 2032. The company also repurchased 75,000 shares of its Class A common stock and paid $118.4 million in dividends to Class A common stockholders. The equity repurchase program was extended to December 31, 2025, with $309 million remaining authorized for repurchases.
  • Future Outlook: Red Rock Resorts plans to allocate approximately $375 million to $425 million for capital expenditures in 2025, focusing on property operations and maintenance. The company anticipates continued debt service payments and dividends, with a quarterly cash dividend of $0.25 per share announced for March 2025. The company expects to fund its operations and capital requirements through cash on hand, cash generated from operations, and borrowings under its credit facilities. Additionally, Red Rock is managing interest rate exposure with interest rate collars and expects to continue its strategic focus on operational efficiencies and cost control.

Challenges and Risks

Red Rock Resorts, Inc. faces several challenges and risks that could impact its operations and financial performance:

  • Market Concentration: The company's concentration in the Las Vegas market subjects it to risks associated with local economic conditions, changes in local and state governmental laws, and natural disasters. The company's strategy of growth through master-planning is based on projected population growth in Las Vegas, which may not continue at its historic pace, potentially affecting future development and expansion plans.
  • Competitive and Regulatory Risks: The gaming industry is highly competitive, with new entrants and expansions by existing competitors potentially impacting market share. Regulatory risks are also significant, with extensive federal, state, and local regulations governing operations. The company is subject to potential increases in gaming taxes and must comply with anti-money laundering regulations, which could result in substantial penalties if violated.
  • Operational Risks: The company is exposed to operational risks such as labor shortages, increased labor costs due to inflation, and union organization activities that could disrupt operations. Construction projects, such as the Durango expansion and North Fork Project, carry risks of delays and cost overruns due to supply chain issues and other unforeseen challenges.
  • Environmental and Technological Risks: Environmental regulations and the evolving regulatory landscape regarding greenhouse gas emissions could impose additional costs and operational challenges. The company's reliance on technology and data security presents risks of cyberattacks and data breaches, which could adversely affect operations and reputation.
  • Economic and Market Risks: Management has identified inflation and rising interest rates as significant challenges impacting consumer discretionary spending, which could affect revenue. The company has implemented cost reduction measures and business optimization strategies to mitigate these impacts. However, the future economic outlook remains uncertain, and management cannot predict the extent to which these factors will affect the business.
  • Financial Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations that could increase borrowing costs. The substantial amount of indebtedness requires significant principal and interest payments, which could limit financial flexibility and ability to respond to changes in the business environment. The company may need to refinance existing debt or seek additional capital, which may not be available on favorable terms.

SEC Filing: Red Rock Resorts, Inc. [ RRR ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Orchid Island Capital, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Orchid Island Capital, Inc., a real estate investment trust (REIT) specializing in residential mortgage-backed securities (RMBS) guaranteed by government-sponsored entities (GSEs) and the Government National Mortgage Association (Ginnie Mae), has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights a significant financial turnaround, strategic capital management, and ongoing efforts to navigate market challenges.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $37.7 million, a substantial improvement from the net loss of $39.2 million in 2023 and $258.5 million in 2022.
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.57, compared to a net loss per share of $0.89 in 2023 and $6.90 in 2022.
  • Interest Income: $241.6 million, an increase of $64.0 million due to a 97 basis points increase in yield on average RMBS and a $453.0 million increase in average RMBS.
  • Interest Expense: $236.3 million, an increase of $34.4 million due to a 28 basis points increase in the average cost of funds and a $428.4 million increase in average borrowings.
  • Net Interest Income: $5.3 million, a turnaround from the net interest expense of $24.4 million in 2023.
  • Gains on RMBS and Derivative Contracts: $49.1 million, a notable improvement from the $3.7 million in 2023 and a loss of $323.9 million in 2022.
  • Expenses: $16.7 million, a decrease from $18.5 million in 2023, reflecting cost management efforts.

Business Highlights

  • Investment Strategy: Focuses on investing in RMBS guaranteed by GSEs and Ginnie Mae, strategically allocating capital between pass-through Agency RMBS and structured Agency RMBS to provide attractive risk-adjusted returns.
  • Portfolio Composition: Consists of traditional pass-through Agency RMBS, such as mortgage pass-through certificates and collateralized mortgage obligations (CMOs), and structured Agency RMBS, including interest-only securities (IOs), inverse interest-only securities (IIOs), and principal-only securities (POs).
  • Risk Management: Employs various strategies, including interest rate swaps, futures contracts, and other derivative instruments, to hedge against interest rate risks and manage prepayment risks associated with its RMBS portfolio.
  • Capital Allocation Strategy: Actively manages the percentage of capital invested in pass-through and structured Agency RMBS to maintain income stability and portfolio value, leveraging pass-through Agency RMBS and maintaining unleveraged structured Agency RMBS to enhance liquidity and diversify interest rate risk.
  • Financing Strategy: Funds RMBS investments primarily through short-term repurchase agreements, with a leverage ratio generally expected to be less than 12 to 1, and considers other sources of leverage, such as secured or unsecured debt and preferred stock issuances.
  • Tax Structure: Operates as a REIT, distributing at least 90% of its REIT taxable income to shareholders to maintain its REIT status.
  • Manager Relationship: Externally managed by Bimini Advisors, LLC, leveraging their expertise in managing Agency RMBS investments and benefiting from their established portfolio management resources.
  • Future Outlook: Intends to continue modifying its investment strategy in response to changing market conditions to maximize returns, maintain its REIT qualification, and focus on risk-adjusted returns and income stability.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Capital Allocation: Focused on maintaining income stability and enhancing liquidity by strategically allocating capital between two categories of Agency RMBS.
  • Capital Raising Activities: Engaged in equity distribution agreements, raising approximately $228.8 million in 2023 and $164.9 million in 2024.
  • Stock Repurchase Program: Authorized to repurchase up to 6,183,601 shares, with 5,144,602 shares repurchased as of December 31, 2024.
  • Liquidity Position: Maintains a robust liquidity position with cash balances, unencumbered assets, and the ability to liquidate encumbered securities.
  • Future Outlook: Plans to continue leveraging repurchase agreement facilities and explore further equity distribution opportunities to support growth and operational stability, while navigating the evolving economic landscape.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: Potential increases in interest rates could negatively impact the value of investments and increase borrowing costs, leading to reduced earnings or losses. Interest rate mismatches between Agency RMBS and borrowings could reduce net interest margins during periods of changing interest rates.
  • Operational Risks: Reliance on repurchase agreement financing, where failure to procure adequate financing or renew existing agreements could adversely affect operations and ability to make distributions. Volatile market conditions for mortgages and mortgage-related assets can lead to liquidity contractions and affect asset values.
  • Regulatory Risks: Potential changes in government policies, laws, and plans in response to geopolitical events, global pandemics, or economic conditions could impact operations. The company must maintain its exemption from regulation under the Investment Company Act and avoid being classified as a commodity pool operator to prevent additional regulatory burdens.
  • Emerging Risks: Potential impact of the Fed's actions on Agency RMBS supply and pricing, reliance on analytical models and data which may be incorrect or misleading, and vulnerability to cyber threats and system failures which could disrupt operations.
  • Management Strategies: Employs hedging strategies to mitigate interest rate risks, maintains diverse financing sources, and carefully manages leverage to mitigate operational risks. However, the effectiveness of these strategies is subject to market conditions and regulatory changes.

SEC Filing: Orchid Island Capital, Inc. [ ORC ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: COMSTOCK RESOURCES INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Comstock Resources, Inc., a leading natural gas producer operating primarily in the Haynesville and Bossier shale plays, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Natural Gas and Oil Sales: $1,047.5 million, a decrease of 17% from 2023, primarily due to lower prices received for natural gas production.
  • Net Income: $(218.8) million, primarily impacted by lower natural gas prices and unrealized losses on derivative financial instruments.
  • Net Income Per Share: $(0.76) per diluted share, reflecting the net loss available to common stockholders.
  • Loss from Operations: $(168.6) million, compared to income from operations of $226.6 million in 2023, driven by lower natural gas prices.

Business Highlights

  • High Quality Properties: The company operates primarily in the Haynesville and Bossier shale plays, with 1,099,090 acres (819,489 net) prospective for development. These properties are located in North Louisiana and East Texas, offering extensive development and exploration potential due to advances in drilling and completion technology.
  • Sales Units: In 2024, the company produced 527,548 MMcf of natural gas and 50 MBbls of oil, reflecting a slight increase in natural gas production compared to the previous year.
  • Successful Drilling Program: The company drilled 50 wells in 2024, with an average lateral length of approximately 10,759 feet, replacing 101% of its 2024 production based on proved reserves added in the SEC price case.
  • Proximity to Premium Markets: The company's natural gas production benefits from strong regional Gulf Coast demand growth, driven by LNG exports and new petrochemical facilities, allowing for higher net realized prices.
  • Company-owned Midstream: Pinnacle Gas Services LLC was formed to provide gathering and treating services, including a gas treating plant and 246 miles of high-pressure pipelines, enhancing operational efficiency.
  • Organic Drilling Inventory Growth: The company focuses on growing its inventory of drilling locations organically, acquiring approximately 517,624 net undeveloped acres over the last five years.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to drill an additional 20 Haynesville and Bossier shale wells in 2025, with a focus on maintaining a conservative operating plan to protect its balance sheet.
  • Environmental Stewardship: The company achieved certification under the MiQ standard for methane emissions, demonstrating a commitment to providing responsibly sourced natural gas.
  • Manage Commodity Price Exposure: The company maintains an active natural gas price hedging program and has access to gas storage in Western Haynesville to enhance operational flexibility.
  • Geographical Performance: All proved reserves are located in the Haynesville and Bossier shales in North Louisiana and East Texas, with wells producing from depths of 10,500 to 19,000 feet.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Debt Management: In 2024, the company issued $400 million in 6.75% senior notes due 2029, using the proceeds to pay down outstanding borrowings on its bank credit facility.
  • Midstream Partnership: The company formed a midstream partnership to fund the build-out of its Western Haynesville midstream system, receiving contributions from a noncontrolling partner.
  • Equity Issuance: The company issued 12.5 million shares of common stock in a private placement to entities controlled by its majority stockholder, raising $100.5 million.
  • Capital Management: The company managed its capital by maintaining a bank credit facility with $1.5 billion in aggregate commitments and a borrowing base of $2.0 billion. As of December 31, 2024, $415 million was outstanding under this facility.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Comstock Resources plans to spend approximately $1.0 billion to $1.1 billion in 2025 on development and exploration projects, primarily in the Haynesville/Bossier shale properties. The company also expects to invest $130 million to $150 million in its Western Haynesville midstream partnership. The company aims to fund these activities through future operating cash flow, with flexibility to adjust capital expenditures as needed.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: Prolonged low natural gas prices could adversely affect revenues, profitability, and cash flow. The company's ability to meet capital expenditure obligations and financial commitments is heavily dependent on natural gas prices.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces challenges in replacing reserves and requires significant capital for exploration and development activities. There is no assurance of successful drilling or acquisition activities.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with environmental laws and regulations could incur substantial liabilities. Changes in regulations, such as those related to climate change and greenhouse gas emissions, could increase operational costs and affect demand for natural gas.
  • Emerging Risks: Increasing scrutiny and changing expectations regarding environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices may impose additional costs and affect access to capital. The company also faces risks related to cybersecurity threats and the potential impact of climate change on its operations.
  • Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by volatile natural gas prices and the need for substantial capital investment to maintain and grow production. The company plans to focus on organic growth through exploration and development in the Haynesville and Bossier shales. Management also highlights the importance of maintaining a disciplined financial strategy to protect the balance sheet and fund operations through cash flow.

SEC Filing: COMSTOCK RESOURCES INC [ CRK ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Bybit Hack Aftermath: Single Whale Liquidated for $46M as BTC Dumps by $4K
data.description:
As the details around perhaps the largest hack in the cryptocurrency industry continue to unravel, BTC’s price has started to crash once again, falling by over four grand since today’s peak.

With the altcoins in a similar state, it’s no wonder that the total value of liquidated positions has skyrocketed to over $600 million on a daily scale.

Recall theeventsthat took place earlier today when reports emerged about suspicious transfers made from Bybit’s hot wallets. Later on, it was confirmed by the company’s CEO, Ben Zhou, that the incident was indeed a hack that had drained its wallet with around $1.4 billion in ETH.

Despite claiming that the exchange, which is one of the largest in terms of global trading volumes, is still solvent, he later admitted that the team had registered “massive withdrawal” requests after the incident.

“It seems that Bybit ‘s ETH multisig cold wallet was compromised through a deceptive transaction that tricked signers into unknowingly approving a malicious smart contract logic change,” – explained the security company Cyvers.

In another message to CryptoPotato, Cyvers’ team said the attacker deployed a malicious contract two days before the hack to the Bybit signers’ devices. When trying to initiate legitimate transactions, the malware acts like the middleman and sends a malicious payload to the hardware wallets. Bybit’s team signs that payload and doesn’t see it due to “blind signing on hardware wallets.”

The attacker then reimplements the safe wallet and delegates the calls to their malicious contract, at which point no additional signatures are needed, and the perpetrator controls the wallet.

The hack, which is described as the largest in the crypto industry, had a dramatic effect on the entire market. BTC had climbed toward $100,000 but was quickly rejected and pushed south by two grand.

After an initial bounce-off, the asset reverted its trajectory and slumped to $95,000, thus losing over $4,000 since the attack. The altcoins suffered even more, with XRP, DOGE, and ADA down by over 6% within the past 24 hours.

The liquidations have shot up to more than $600 million, according to CoinGlass. The single-largest wrecked order took place on HTC and was worth a whopping $45.8 million.

data.title: Trump Plans to Visit Fort Knox's Gold Reserve. Selling It Won't Fix the U.S. Budget. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Paul R. La Monica

The United States is sitting on a pile of gold that would make Scrooge McDuck envious, especially now that the yellow metal is trading near all-time highs. But selling it wouldn't do much to help the federal government pay its bills.

First, let's get the speculation and rumors about gold out of the way.

Some are questioning just how much gold the U.S. actually owns after Elon Musk, the Tesla CEO and " special government employee" who is helping President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), posted the following on X (which Musk owns) earlier this week.

"Who is confirming that gold wasn't stolen from Fort Knox? Maybe it's there, maybe it's not," Musk wrote. "That gold is owned by the American public! We want to know if it's still there."

Trump responded to that post in an interview on Air Force One, saying "We're going to go into Fort Knox, the fabled Fort Knox, to make sure the gold is there. He added that "if the gold isn't there, we're going to be very upset." Trump then said Thursday that he plans to go to Fort Knox at some point. Musk posted a video of that interview on X, joking that a livestream of Fort Knox would be the equivalent of two fire emojis.

Here are the facts. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said earlier this week in an interview with conservative talk show host Dan O'Donnell from the White House that "we do an audit every year" of Fort Knox and added that "all the gold is present and accounted for."

So how much gold is there? The U.S. has 147.3 million ounces stored at Fort Knox in Kentucky. Based on current prices of just under $2,950 an ounce, that is worth about $435 billion. But that is only a little more than half of the government's gold stash.

According to the Federal Reserve, the U.S. currently holds about 261.5 million ounces of gold overall, with sizable portions in deep storage in vaults in Denver and West Point as well as a chunk at the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. (Cue the scene of robbers stealing the NY Fed's gold in Die Hard With a Vengeance.) So the total amount of U.S.-owned gold is worth a little more than $770 billion at current prices.

But here's where things get a little tricky. The book value for the government's gold stake is just $42.22 an ounce. That dates back to the 1970s when President Richard Nixon's administration set that price for gold as a way to weaken the dollar and curb inflation. It effectively ended the gold standard, the monetary system that pegged the dollar directly to gold.

Bessent said in an interview with Bloomberg this week that there were no plans to revalue the nation's gold holdings.

In fact, changing it so that the gold would be marked to market can only be done through an act of Congress. Three Republican House members introduced a bill in 2023 to revalue gold at current prices. It's unclear whether this will once again gain traction.

Let's assume for the sake of argument that Congress does allow gold to be revalued at current prices. In that hypothetical scenario, the Fed's gold stake, which is now "worth" only about $11 million at $42.22, would suddenly jump more than $750 billion in value on its balance sheet at current market prices.

The U.S. wouldn't actually need to sell gold to pay its bills. The windfall on paper could give the White House and Congress some wiggle room to negotiate a new budget and extend the debt ceiling so that the government could sell more bonds to pay bills and fund federal programs.

"A revaluation of the gold can technically provide the Treasury with additional cash and could have significant near-term market implications if Congress decides to pursue this policy," said strategists at TD Securities in a report Thursday.

It's sort of like taking out a home equity line of credit to free up more money if the value of your house has gone up since you bought it.

David Miller, chief investment officer and senior portfolio manager at Catalyst Funds, told Barron's that selling the gold outright at current prices makes sense. That way, Washington could use proceeds to cut interest payments on its debt. "The only reason for holding the gold is a relic of the gold standard," he said.

The TD strategists added that by marking its gold assets to market, the U.S. would be able to extend the so-called X date, which is when the Treasury would run out of cash, from sometime in August to early 2026.

"This could prove especially useful as Congress attempts to negotiate a renewal of the 2017 tax cuts and deliver on President Trump's other fiscal priorities," the TD strategists said.

But it wouldn't be a true game changer. Consider that the total U.S. debt load now stands at $36.22 trillion.

"It's a drop in the bucket compared to our debt," said George Milling-Stanley, chief gold strategist at State Street Global Advisors. "The notion we might revalue or sell our gold and pay our debt has come up from time to time but there's nowhere near as much gold in our reserves to do that."

There's not enough gold on the planet to completely pay off the debt. According to the World Gold Council, the current value of all gold globally is $20.5 trillion.

Write to Paul R. La Monica at paul.lamonica@barrons.com

data.title: CIBC's Week Ahead Market Call For Canada
data.description:
According to Ali Jaffery and Katherine Judge over at CIBC, all eyes will be on the GDP data next Friday, with the fourth quarter "set to show an acceleration" on a boost to activity from the housing market, solid consumption tied to the GST holiday, and growth in exports helped by tariff front running. The CIBC duo said even though the January advanced data will likely show a "healthy" start to the year, they expect momentum to fade over the first quarter as tariff uncertainty weighs, which should leave the Bank of Canada on a path towards an overnight rate of 2.25%.

(Also on the CIBC calendar for next week are an appearance by Bank of Canada Deputy Governor, Toni Gravelle, on a panel in London, UK, at lunchtime on Monday. In terms of data, Q4 Current Account and December Payrolls data is due out Thursday.)

data.title: Sibanye Stillwater Narrows Annual Net Loss in 2024 as Revenue Slips; Shares Decline
data.description:
Sibanye Stillwater reported Friday a 2024 net loss of $0.14 per share compared with a $0.72 per share loss for 2023.

Two analysts polled by FactSet expected an annual loss of $0.51.

Revenue for the 12 months ended Dec. 31 was $6.12 billion compared with $6.17 billion for 2023.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $5.82 billion.

Shares were down nearly 6% in recent afternoon trading.

data.title: Sector Update: Health Care
data.description:
Health care stocks were softer late Friday afternoon with the NYSE Health Care Index easing 0.2% and the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) down 0.3%.

The iShares Biotechnology ETF (IBB) was fractionally lower.

In corporate news, Estrella Immunopharma shares climbed 3.4% after it said Friday it has completed the first dose group in an ongoing phase 1/2 trial assessing EB103 in adults with relapsed/refractory B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma.

data.title: ICE Review: Canola Mixed, Ends Week Higher
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--The ICE Futures canola market was mixed despite mostly negative sentiment in comparable oils.

Malaysian palm oil was positive Friday, but Chicago soyoil and European rapeseed prices were lower. Crude oil lost more than $1 per barrel because of rising U.S. stockpiles and refinery maintenance.

The Canadian Grain Commission reported 172,800 metric tons of canola exported during the week ended Feb. 16. Cumulative exports this marketing year total 5.845 million tons compared to 3.17 million one year ago.

One analyst said he is becoming hesitant to put more funds into canola because of uncertainty over tariffs. He also said there needs to be more demand rationing in the Canadian market.

The Canadian dollar was down two-tenths of a U.S. cent compared to Thursday's close.

There were 56,032 contracts traded on Friday, which compares with Thursday when 43,197 contracts changed hands. Spreading accounted for 42,404 of the contracts traded.

Settlement prices are in Canadian dollars per metric ton

data.title: Bitcoin comes back to life — Does data support a rally to $100K and higher?
data.description:
Bitcoin reached $99,500 on Feb. 21, its highest level in two weeks, but failed to maintain the bullish momentum. This move can be partly attributed to a lack of enthusiasm in Bitcoin's derivatives markets. Traders have been reluctant to open bullish positions since the rejection at $102,000 on Feb. 3.

The Bitcoin futures premium typically fluctuates between 5% and 10% in neutral markets, compensating for the longer settlement period. However, this indicator has not shown bullish momentum since Feb. 3, and the recent ris

e from $95,500 on Feb. 19 to $99,500 on Feb. 21 was insufficient to break the trend.

Bitcoin price surged briefly due to China’s adjusted M1 supply data

Investor optimism was briefly sparked by a surprising increase in China’s broad M1 monetary supply data, which showed a significant jump in January. However, this movement was misinterpreted, as the methodology was adjusted to include individual checking accounts and holdings in non-bank payment platforms such as Alipay and WeChat Pay.

Credit growth in China accelerated, with data released on Feb. 14 showing new loans from financial institutions rising by $702 billion in January, the highest level since 1992. Michelle Lam, Greater China economist at Societe Generale, stated that the latest data “suggests policymakers are adding fuel to the economy," according to Yahoo Finance.

To assess whether the recent Bitcoin price gains have influenced the sentiment of whales and market makers, it is essential to analyze the BTC options markets. If traders anticipate a correction, put (sell) options will trade at a premium, pushing the 25% delta skew metric above 6%. Conversely, periods of bullishness cause the indicator to move below -6%.

The Bitcoin options market showed little excitement over the recent $99,500 retest, as the 25% delta skew indicator remained at 5%, within the neutral range. Notably, the last instance of bullishness, based on this metric, occurred on Jan. 26, when Bitcoin’s price approached $105,000.

To gain a broader understanding of cryptocurrency demand, one can look at the stablecoin market. Typically, strong interest in cryptocurrencies in China causes stablecoins to trade at a premium of 2% or more above the official US dollar rate. In contrast, a discount often indicates fear as traders rush to exit the crypto markets.

The USDT premium in China has remained close to 0.5% for the past week, within the neutral range. Similar to other Bitcoin derivatives metrics, the last time stablecoins traded at a 2% premium in China was on Feb. 3, indicating that traders were largely unfazed by the recent price movement toward $100,000.

Mixed emotions following President Trump’s cabinet picks

Traders’ reduced demand reflects two consecutive weeks of failed attempts to maintain levels above $98,000, along with some disappointment over President Trump's crypto council, which has reportedly been canceled as the administration chose to hold informal summits instead. Similarly, there has been excessive hype around the potential creation of a strategic Bitcoin reserve.

On a positive note, the US Securities and Exchange Commission announced its intention to drop charges against Coinbase, signaling a favorable regulatory environment. Additionally, Howard Lutnick, former CEO of Cantor Fitzgerald, was confirmed as the US Secretary of Commerce. Lutnick is a vocal Bitcoin supporter, which boosts traders’ hopes for accelerated institutional adoption.

Despite the relatively low interest in Bitcoin, a constructive scenario for an all-time high remains in place as investors gradually recognize the cryptocurrency's hedge against inflation and censorship-resistant qualities.

This article is for general information purposes and is not intended to be and should not be taken as legal or investment advice. The views, thoughts, and opinions expressed here are the author’s alone and do not necessarily reflect or represent the views and opinions of Cointelegraph.

data.title: Itron Executives Sell Shares to Cover Tax Obligations
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Deitrich ThomasPresident & CEOSell$2,771,512Form 4
Hooper Joan SSVP & CFOSell$899,452Form 4
Marcolini John F.SVP, Networked SolutionsSell$329,001Form 4
Patrick Justin KSVP, Device SolutionsSell$160,220Form 4

Several executives at Itron, including the President & CEO, CFO, and SVPs of Networked Solutions and Device Solutions, have sold shares of common stock to cover tax withholding obligations associated with the vesting of performance-based restricted stock unit awards.

Thomas Deitrich, President & CEO, sold 28,490 shares at $97.2802 per share, totaling $2,771,512. Following this transaction, Deitrich directly owns 271,489 shares and indirectly owns 25,000 shares through a trust.

Joan S. Hooper, SVP & CFO, sold 9,246 shares at $97.2802 per share, totaling $899,452. After the sale, Hooper directly owns 94,398 shares.

John F. Marcolini, SVP of Networked Solutions, sold 3,382 shares at $97.2802 per share, totaling $329,001. Marcolini directly owns 24,606 shares following the transaction.

Patrick Justin K, SVP of Device Solutions, sold 1,647 shares at $97.2802 per share, totaling $160,220. Patrick directly owns 31,531 shares after the sale.

data.title: Sector Update: Financial
data.description:
Financial stocks dropped in late Friday afternoon trading with the NYSE Financial Index falling 1.3% and the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) down 1.1%.

The Philadelphia Housing Index declined 2.4%, and the Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLRE) shed 0.8%.

Bitcoin (BTC/USD) fell 3.2% to $95,274, and the yield for 10-year US Treasuries dropped 8 basis points to 4.42%.

In economic news, the University of Michigan consumer sentiment index was revised downward on Friday to 64.7 for February from 67.8 in the preliminary estimate, compared with expectations for no revision in a survey compiled by Bloomberg.

Output growth in the US private sector unexpectedly slowed in February as the services sector contracted, while optimism waned amid concern over the impact of tariffs and domestic spending cuts, S&P Global's (SPGI) flash purchasing managers' index showed. The composite output gauge fell to a 17-month low of 50.4 from 52.7 in January, compared with the 53.2 consensus in a survey compiled by Bloomberg.

In corporate news, Block shares fell 17%, a day after the company reported Q4 adjusted earnings and revenue that missed estimates by analysts.
